title: Nuance and precision
canDo: I can refine and qualify my meaning precisely.

chunks.example: It's a subtle distinction, but it matters.

chunks.text: To put it another way, it's risky.
chunks.example: To put it another way, it's risky.
More precisely, it doubled overnight.
Broadly speaking, the approach works.
If anything, it's improved.

grammar.pattern: To put it another way... / More precisely... / Broadly speaking...

reading.passage: People say the policy failed, but it's a subtle distinction. To put it another way, the goal changed midway. More precisely, results improved in one area while falling in another. Broadly speaking, it was mixed. If anything, public trust has grown.

questions.q: How does the writer describe the outcome?
What grew, according to the writer?

script.t: It's a subtle distinction. Broadly speaking, it was mixed.

gist.q: Does B say it simply failed?

gap.q: To put it ___ way, it's risky.

fluency432.prompt: Explain a complex situation, qualifying your statements.

retell.prompt: Summarise the nuanced view of the policy.

task.prompt: Give a nuanced opinion on a topic, qualifying as you go.

writing.model: It's a subtle distinction. To put it another way, the aim shifted. Broadly speaking, the result was mixed; if anything, trust improved.

sentenceCombining.answer: Because the aim changed, the result was mixed.
It did not fail; if anything, it improved.

task.prompt: Write a nuanced paragraph (4 sentences) qualifying a strong claim.

quiz.q: Rephrase to make a point clearer.
options: To put it another way, it's risky.

title: Academic discourse
canDo: I can present and interpret evidence in formal English.

chunks.text: This suggests that demand is rising.
chunks.example: This suggests that demand is rising.

chunks.text: The evidence indicates a clear trend.
chunks.example: The evidence indicates a clear trend.

chunks.text: It is widely accepted that sleep matters.
chunks.example: It is widely accepted that sleep matters.

chunks.text: with the exception of one case
chunks.example: All passed, with the exception of one case.
In terms of cost, it is efficient.

grammar.pattern: verb to noun: analyse to the analysis; implement to implementation
examples: The analysis of the data shows...

reading.passage: The study examined sleep and memory. The evidence indicates a clear trend: more sleep, better recall. This suggests that schools should start later. It is widely accepted that sleep matters, with the exception of a few disputed cases. In terms of policy, the analysis is persuasive.

questions.q: What does the writer suggest schools do?

script.t: The evidence indicates a clear trend; this suggests demand is rising.

dictogloss.sentence: The evidence indicates a clear trend.

fluency432.prompt: Present a finding and what it suggests.

task.prompt: Present a piece of evidence and interpret it cautiously.

writing.model: The evidence indicates a clear trend. This suggests that earlier interventions help. It is widely accepted that, in terms of outcomes, prevention is cheaper than cure.

sentenceCombining.answer: The analysis of the data produced clear results.
The evidence indicates that demand is rising.

task.prompt: Write a formal paragraph (4 sentences) presenting and interpreting evidence.

options: This suggests that demand is rising.
with the exception of one case
It is widely accepted that sleep matters.

title: Diplomatic English
canDo: I can disagree diplomatically and steer a discussion.

chunks.example: I take your point, but the cost is high.
With respect, I disagree.
Could we revisit this next week?
Let's park that for now.
I'll circle back to you tomorrow.

grammar.pattern: I take your point, but... / With respect, ... / Let's park that...
examples: I see what you mean, but...
Could we come back to this?

reading.passage: In the meeting, Sara said: I take your point about speed, but with respect, I disagree on the budget. Could we revisit this once we have numbers? Let's park that for now and move on. I'll circle back to you with figures by Friday.

script.t: I take your point, but with respect, I disagree.

gist.q: Does B agree to cut the budget?

fluency432.prompt: Disagree with a proposal diplomatically and suggest a next step.

retell.prompt: Summarise how Sara handled the meeting.

task.prompt: Disagree with a colleague's idea politely and propose revisiting it.

writing.model: I take your point about speed, but with respect, I disagree on the budget. Could we revisit this with data? I'll circle back by Friday.

sentenceCombining.answer: I take your point, but the cost is too high.
Let's not decide now; let's park it for the moment.

task.prompt: Write a diplomatic reply (4 sentences) disagreeing and proposing a next step.

title: Inversion for emphasis
canDo: I can use inversion to add emphasis in speech and writing.

chunks.text: Not only is it cheap, but it's fast.
chunks.example: Not only is it cheap, but it's fast.

chunks.text: Never have I seen such talent.
chunks.example: Never have I seen such talent.

chunks.text: Rarely do we find such quality.
chunks.example: Rarely do we find such quality.

chunks.text: Little did I know what would happen.
chunks.example: Little did I know what would happen.

chunks.text: Only then did I realise the truth.
chunks.example: Only then did I realise the truth.

grammar.focus: Inversion after negative or limiting adverbs
grammar.pattern: Never have I... / Rarely do we... / Not only is it...
examples: Never had he tried so hard.

reading.passage: The launch went better than anyone expected. Not only was the product cheap, but it was fast. Never have I seen such interest. Rarely do we find a team so motivated. Little did we know it would sell out, and only then did we realise we needed more stock.

questions.q: What did they realise too late?

choices: we did not realise at the time

script.t: Never have I seen such talent!

dictogloss.sentence: Rarely do we find such quality.

gap.q: Only then ___ I realise the truth.

fluency432.prompt: Describe an impressive event, using emphasis.

retell.prompt: Retell the launch using emphatic inversion.

task.prompt: Describe a memorable experience using at least two inversions.

writing.model: Not only was the launch cheap, but it was fast. Never have we seen such interest. Only then did we realise we needed more stock.

sentenceCombining.answer: Not only is it cheap, but it is also fast.

parts: I had never seen such talent.
sentenceCombining.answer: Never had I seen such talent, and I was amazed.

task.prompt: Write 3-4 emphatic sentences about a success, using inversion.

options: Never have I seen such talent.
Only then did I realise the truth.

quiz.q: Not ___ is it cheap, but it's fast.

title: Hedging
canDo: I can soften claims and hedge appropriately.

chunks.text: It may well be the case.
chunks.example: It may well be the case.

chunks.text: This tends to suggest a link.
chunks.example: This tends to suggest a link.
It's true, to a certain degree.
Arguably, it's the best option.
On balance, it's worth it.

grammar.pattern: may well / tends to / to a certain degree / arguably

reading.passage: The results are promising, but we should be careful. It may well be the case that the effect is real. This tends to suggest a link, though only to a certain degree. Arguably, more research is needed. On balance, the early signs are encouraging.

questions.q: What does the writer say is needed?

script.t: It may well be the case, but arguably we need more data.

fluency432.prompt: Give a cautious assessment of a new idea.

retell.prompt: Summarise the cautious view of the results.

task.prompt: Assess a claim cautiously, hedging where appropriate.

writing.model: It may well be the case that the effect is real. This tends to suggest a link, to a certain degree. Arguably, more research is needed; on balance, the signs are good.

sentenceCombining.answer: The effect may well be real, although we need more data.

parts: It works to a certain degree.
sentenceCombining.answer: It works, at least to a certain degree.

task.prompt: Write a cautious paragraph (4 sentences) hedging your claims.

options: It may well be the case.

title: Idiom and register
canDo: I can use idioms and shift register appropriately.

chunks.example: Let's cut to the chase.
It's a grey area, legally.
That's a game changer.
In a nutshell, we won.
The bottom line is cost.

examples: cut to the chase / get to the point

reading.passage: At the briefing, the manager said: let's cut to the chase. The new tool is a game changer, but the rules are a grey area. In a nutshell, it could save us a fortune. The bottom line is cost, so we will test it carefully before rolling it out.

questions.q: How does the manager describe the new tool?

script.t: In a nutshell, it's a game changer.

minimalPair.note: same word, two spellings; identical sound /greɪ/ — focus on the clear long /eɪ/.

fluency432.prompt: Summarise a decision using a couple of idioms.

retell.prompt: Summarise the briefing using the idioms.

task.prompt: Brief a colleague on an update, using two idioms and getting to the point.

writing.model: Let's cut to the chase. The new tool is a game changer, though the rules are a grey area. In a nutshell, the bottom line is cost.

sentenceCombining.answer: Let's cut to the chase: the tool is a game changer.
The rules are a grey area, so we must be careful.

task.prompt: Write a brief update (4 sentences) using two idioms appropriately.

quiz.q: State the single most important point.
